page: Amazon Web Services r7i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The configuration provides 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This high-speed memory configuration delivers a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1379.96 GB/sec and an uncached large block read-only bandwidth of 100.58 GB/sec. There is no local NVMe or SSD storage bundled with this instance type, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance testing indicates strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2141.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.78 GB/sec. Text processing performance is highly efficient, reaching a top-tier Geekbench text processing score of 2740.0. In multi-core operations, the server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 26176.98 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 15858.69. For database workloads, the instance processed 21.24 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 8071.72 in PassMark database operations. Small-model LLM text generation reached a top-tier speed of 333.12 tokens/sec.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and DDR5 memory architecture, the r7i.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local storage. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage configurations. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and small to medium scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but includes no local storage. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2141.0 and top-tier text processing and small-model LLM text generation speeds. Multi-core performance is solid, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 26176.98 and 21.24 million Redis SET operations per second. This profile makes the r7i.4xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and enterprise applications requiring high memory bandwidth without local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive databases and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, but no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized, x86-64 server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support.
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation.
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications.
